items.title: Should You Buy Stocks Before the End of the Year? Here's What History Says
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The article discusses the potential benefits of investing in December and January, including the January Effect and the impact of falling interest rates and optimism about Trump's economic policies. However, it cautions investors to be cautious and stick to safer, reasonably valued stocks in the current market environment.

items.title: The Stock Market Is Doing Something It Has Done Only 2 Times Since 1985, and History Is Clear About What Happens Next
items.author: Trevor Jennewine
items.description: The S&P 500 has reached a valuation level seen only twice since 1985, and both times the index eventually crashed. The current high valuation is driven by the 10 largest stocks, leaving the remaining 490 stocks more attractively priced.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article cites FactSet Research as a source for data on the S&P 500's valuation.

The article highlights Goldman Sachs' analysis that an equal-weight S&P 500 index fund could outperform the traditional market-cap-weighted S&P 500 by 5 percentage points per year over the next decade.
